# Overdrag scrolling never updates the SortZone scroll term ŌĆö post-scroll drag math corrupts

## Context

Sub-issue of #12883. Operator manual repro on merged dev (post `#12901` trace instrumentation), full-tail trace captured live from his session: dragging a column right until auto-scroll engages, then dragging back, produces (a) body cells that stop updating and (b) header cells moving wrongly. The trace convicts one mechanism for both.

`Release classification:` parent #12883 carries the v13 board gate; this sub is its PR-deliverable leaf.

## The Problem

Trace evidence (operator session, single drag of `privateContributionsRatio`):

1. Seven `scroll` events fire (the engine auto-scrolls ~6 columns; the container physically moves) ŌĆö **and `sl: 0` on every event in the entire trace.** The zone's scroll-correction term (`me.scrollLeft`, consumed by the move-delta math `delta = clientX - ownerX + me.scrollLeft - offsetX - itemRects[i].left`) never updates.
2. The moment the drag returns in-bounds, deltas read `d: -812, -769, -700, ŌĆ”` (pointer at viewport ~1276 vs full-span slot positions 1586+ with zero correction) ŌåÆ **an avalanche of wrong switches, index 16ŌåÆ0 in ~250ms** ŌĆö the operator's "header cells do not move correctly".
3. The same uncorrected coordinates drive `switchItems`' body-cell/columnPositions writes mid-scroll while the engine's scroll-triggered re-pooling rebuilds rows ŌĆö two write paths in different coordinate spaces ŌĆö the operator's "body cells do not update".

## The Architectural Reality

- Normal horizontal scroll-sync runs through the `GridHorizontalScrollSync` main-thread addon, registered against the **dedicated horizontalScrollbar element** (`grid.ScrollManager#updateGridHorizontalScrollSyncAddon`); `grid.header.Toolbar#afterSetScrollLeft` then pushes into `sortZone.scrollLeft`.
- The drag's overdrag path scrolls via `grid.header.Toolbar#scrollToIndex` ŌåÆ `Neo.main.DomAccess.scrollIntoView` ŌĆö a programmatic native scroll of the **grid container**, invisible to that pipeline. Nothing writes the zone's term.
- Semantics note: the zone's `scrollLeft` is **scroll-since-drag-start** (the drag-start `itemRects` snapshot bakes in any pre-existing scroll), so the correct source is the owner toolbar's viewport-x shift relative to the drag-start `ownerRect.x` ŌĆö not the absolute container scrollLeft.

## The Fix

1. `draggable.container.SortZone#scrollToIndex`: after the owner scroll completes, re-measure the owner's rect and set `me.scrollLeft = me.ownerRect.x - liveOwnerRect.x` (the viewport shift = scroll-since-start, by construction).
2. Reset `scrollLeft: 0` at drag start and in the drag-end state reset (it currently leaks across drags).
3. Whitebox-e2e repro (operator-proposed): a spec driving overdrag + drag-back via `page.mouse`, asserting through the drag trace ŌĆö `sl > 0` after `scroll` events, no switch avalanche (bounded switch count for the gesture), and post-drop header/body agreement. Requires a small fixture passthrough for `getDragTrace`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After overdrag scrolling, trace `move` events carry `sl` equal to the scrolled amount (non-zero).
- [ ] Dragging back in-bounds after a scroll produces NO avalanche (switches follow the pointer; deltas bounded by pointer movement).
- [ ] Body cells and headers agree during and after the scroll+return gesture (within-region drop lands where the pointer indicates).
- [ ] Whitebox-e2e spec covers the gesture end-to-end via trace assertions and passes.
- [ ] `scrollLeft` does not leak across drags (second drag starts at 0).

## Out of Scope

- Mid-drag user wheel-scrolling (the toolbar's absolute-value push into the zone's relative term is a pre-existing space mismatch; separate ticket if it bites).
- The locked-start-region drag never engaging overdrag at all (separate gap, logged during this investigation).

## Related

- Parent: #12883 ┬Ę Trace instrumentation that enabled the conviction: `#12901`/`#12902` ┬Ę Fixture identity-bind: `#12897`

### @neo-fable - 2026-06-11T15:58:46Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ŌØŗ ## Scope evolution: term-derivation ŌåÆ pipeline routing (operator back-leg report incorporated)
> 
> The committed first fix (`04d465f5d`, the ownerRect-shift readback) cures the avalanche but not the structure. The operator's follow-up repro ŌĆö *overdrag right ~5 columns, then drag back left to index 0 in the same gesture: hard cut at a left position, no overdrag scroll back* ŌĆö **reproduces on the fix branch** and convicts the deeper mechanism.
> 
> ### Live evidence (devindex rig, scroll-surface sampling per phase + drag trace)
> 
> | Surface | pre | overdrag-right hold | back-leg hold | post-drop |
> |---|---|---|---|---|
> | grid container scrollLeft | 0 | 202 ŌåÆ 623 ŌåÆ **1120** | frozen 1120 ŌåÆ 563 ŌåÆ **370 (stuck)** | 0 |
> | locked-start toolbar viewport-x | 17 | **ŌłÆ185 ŌåÆ ŌłÆ1007 ŌåÆ ŌłÆ1103** | ŌłÆ353 | 17 |
> | h-scrollbar scrollLeft (the SSOT) | 0 | **0** | **0** | 0 |
> | body `--grid-scroll-left` / re-render | ŌĆö | **never fires** | never | ŌĆö |
> 
> 1. `scrollIntoView` scrolls the **grid container** (mid-drag the owner toolbar is forced to content width 3036, so the wrapper overflows and the container becomes the nearest scrollable ancestor). The locked regions ride along (operator's "header cells move wrongly"); the scrollbar pipeline ŌĆö the only thing that re-renders body cells and feeds the worker-side scroll state ŌĆö never fires (operator's "body cells do not update").
> 2. **Back-leg hard cut quantified:** `inline:'nearest'` is satisfied once center column 0 is flush with the *grid's* left edge ŌĆö grid scrollLeft 370 = the locked-start width, never recoverable. Trace tail: `scrollSync` frozen at 1120 for 10 beats (targets already "visible" in the scrolled container), then 1005ŌåÆ410 and stuck; the drop then landed `end from:2 to:3` ŌĆö the user aimed at slot 0 and the column moved one slot **right**.
> 3. The `grid.header.Toolbar#afterSetScrollLeft ŌåÆ sortZone.scrollLeft` wiring has **no writer anywhere** ŌĆö the zone term was stale by construction on every path (including mid-drag user wheel scrolling, the hole this ticket's Out-of-Scope had deferred).
> 4. Designing the reroute surfaced a pre-existing drag-independent range bug ŌåÆ #12907: the scrollbar maxes at 1390 while the center toolbar needs 1880 (short by exactly the locked widths; last ~5 devindex columns unreachable by plain user scrolling).
> 
> ### The revised fix (one PR, Resolves this + #12907)
> 
> - **#12907 substrate:** scrollbar scrollport scoped to the center region (margin configs fed from the locked bodies' `availableWidth`, mirroring the `centerWidth` feed) ŌåÆ max scroll = center content ŌłÆ center clip, exact for every region split.
> - **Overdrag reroute:** `Toolbar#scrollToIndex` computes a nearest-target from the slot rect (center-content space) and drives the **scrollbar element** via `DomAccess.scrollTo` ŌĆö the production pipeline does everything else: addon syncs header scrollLeft + body CSS var in-frame; `ScrollManager` ingests; a one-line feed (`headerToolbar.scrollLeft = scrollManager.scrollLeft`) revives the dead wiring so the zone term updates on EVERY scroll source. Locked toolbars' `scrollToIndex` no-ops (`layoutLock` guard).
> - **Owner expansion opt-out:** the grid header zone skips the mid-drag owner width force (new base config, default unchanged) ŌĆö the toolbar stays a real clipped scroller, so the container never becomes a rogue scrollable ancestor; absolutely-positioned drag items extend the toolbar's scrollable overflow, native scrolling keeps working mid-drag.
> - **Trailing-switch guard (residual 3):** post-await `itemRects` re-check in the overdrag branches.
> - The scrolled-drop verdict corruption (residual 2) **dissolves structurally**: the locked bodies never move, so the drag-start `regionRects` snapshot stays valid for the entire gesture.
> 
> AC set unchanged plus the back-leg gesture: drag-back must reach center index 0 with locked columns visible and the body following both directions.

* fix(grid): scope the horizontal scrollbar scrollport to the center region (#12907)

* fix(draggable): route column-drag overdrag scrolling through the scrollbar pipeline (#12906)
